subject: english
learning_objectives: Explain intonation and stress in spoken English
Apply rise-fall and fall-rise patterns in speech

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content:
Intonation and stress are very important in spoken English. The meaning of an utterance is affected, not only by stress, but also by the variations in the pitch of a voice.When one speaks the voice or pitch changes; becomes high or low depending on the meaning the speaker intends.

Note: The meaning of an utterance is affected by variations in pitch.

The sentence intonation patterns are:

sections.heading: Falling Tune
sections.content:
Used in declarative sentences, commands, exclamations, and wh-questions.
a. Statements

Joy works hard in school.

Emeka has given the book to her.


b. Commands

Keep the books on my table

Stand under the tree.


c. Wh – question

Why are your very late?

Who gave you the information?


d. Exclamations

What a good result!

How pretty she is!

sections.heading: Rising Tune
sections.content:
The rising tune is used in

a.	Polar Questions (questions which demand only ‘yes’ or ‘no’ as answer)
            
Is the wine sweet?
   	    
Will Monday be good for it?
   	    
Did you eat last night?

b.	Utterances showing indifference 
            
You can do what you want
            
I think that’s right.

c.	Listing items.
            
One, two, three and four.
            
We need some rice, beans, fish and meat.
            
In enumeration, the final item in the list is usually said on a falling tune. 
            
Although the falling and rising intonation patterns are the two basic pitch patterns, the two
            
Pitch movements can be combined in long sentences.

sections.heading: Rise and Fall Pattern
sections.content:
Examples:
While the boys were working, the girls were playing
When the rain comes, I’ll plant my seed yams

sections.heading: Fall and Rise Pattern
sections.content:
Examples:
The students are happy, aren’t they?
You can do it, can’t you?
